summary: Paper-runner mismatch alerts were traced to a stale local desktop runner from the April 22 staged release plus residual broker option positions.
execution_safety_findings: The sanctioned VM execution path was not observed running the paper session from this machine.
A stale local Windows desktop runner process was active from the April 22 staged release and was stopped.
Legacy local Windows scheduled tasks pointing at the April 22 staged release were disabled to prevent relaunch.
Residual paper broker option positions were flattened under explicit incident remediation authority.
A read-only broker check after remediation reported zero positions and zero open orders.
The sanctioned VM runner source was patched to runner commit f008006 without starting a broker-facing session.
IAP SSH verified no active runner process before deployment.
A later broker check detected new QQQ option exposure and a new MSFT buy-to-open order from an unattributed source.
The new MSFT open order was canceled and the QQQ option exposure was flattened in short-first risk-reducing order.
A 45-second post-flatten recurrence check reported zero broker positions and zero open orders.
A 90-second broker watch after disabling an additional stale launch surface reported zero broker positions, zero open orders, and no newer broker orders.
The Windows scheduled task GovernedDownChoppyTakeoverUser was disabled because it pointed at an older takeover checkout and was inconsistent with the one sanctioned VM execution path.
The remaining Stage27 scheduled tasks were disabled because they were stale local automation outside the sanctioned VM execution path.
A 180-second broker watch after final local task fencing reported zero broker positions, zero open orders, and no newer broker orders.
root_cause: Unexpected-position cleanup processed broker positions in API order.
For option spreads, that can close protective long legs before margin-reducing short covers.
Alpaca rejected the unsafe sequence in at least one cleanup path with an options buying-power error.

code_fix.change: Residual option cleanup now prioritizes short-leg buy-to-close orders before protective long-leg sell-to-close orders for both known-trade cleanup and unexpected broker-position cleanup.

hard_rules_preserved: No strategy selection or risk policy was changed.
No raw session exhaust or raw trade log was committed.
remaining_actions: Keep runner commit f008006 on the sanctioned VM before the next broker-facing session.
Re-run the VM process/source-stamp check immediately before arming any exclusive execution window.
Keep legacy local scheduled tasks disabled; do not use the April 22 staged release as an execution path.
Keep GovernedDownChoppyTakeoverUser disabled; do not use older takeover checkouts as broker-capable launch surfaces.
Keep Stage27 scheduled tasks disabled; do not use stale local Stage27 automation as a broker-capable launch surface.
Treat the unattributed order source as a hard pre-arm blocker until no external runner/process/thread is found or all candidate launch surfaces are disabled.
Before any further session, run a longer broker no-new-order watch and confirm no orders appear without an explicit operator launch.
Use docs/gcp_foundation/gcp_execution_launch_surface_audit_handoff.md as the current launch-surface takeover read before any exclusive-window arm.
